AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Admin.title: Admin Console
AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Admin.description: Apiculus Admin Console is the top level experience management layer in the Apiculus solution stack. This is the control panel where service providers' Product, Marketing and Business teams spend most of their time and manage various facets of the cloud delivery and management experience for the end customers.

AboutServiceProviderAdministration/ISVCo.title: Independent Software Vendor Consoles
AboutServiceProviderAdministration/ISVCo.description: The Apiculus solution is built using an 'integrations' approach using Independent Software Vendor (ISV) products that are enterprise-grade and deliver powerful technical orchestration capabilities.

AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Manag.title: Managing Support Tickets
AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Manag.description: Any request, query or bug report can be raised as a ticket with Apiculus (By Yotta) Support via email. This also includes forwarding tickets raised by the end customers on Apiculus Cloud Console or any other channels permitted by service providers. Apiculus (By Yotta) Support does not respond directly to end-customer tickets and only acts as L2+ escalation.

AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Metab.title: Metabase Business Intelligence Dashboards
AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Metab.description: The Apiculus solution stack includes the open-source BI tool Metabase, which can be configured to deliver powerful business and commercial reporting insights. Service providers' techno-commercial teams (Product, Operations, Business, Upper Management, C-Suite) can configure and access highly customisable visual dashboards to gain visibility into how the cloud business is being operated using Apiculus.

AboutServiceProviderAdministration/OTRST.title: OTRS Ticketing and Service Interface
AboutServiceProviderAdministration/OTRST.description: Apiculus Admin Console enables end-to-end service management via tickets using OTRS Community Edition. Service providers get agent and admin access to OTRS for being able to onboard their helpdesk teams and configure workflows as needed.

AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Role-.title: Role-based Access on Admin Console
AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Role-.description: Apiculus Admin Console offers role-based access control (RBAC) for all admin users. The system ships with default roles, with rights and privileges as outlined in this article.

AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Zabbi.title: Zabbix Dashboard and NMS
AboutServiceProviderAdministration/Zabbi.description: Service provider Network Operations Center (NOC) teams get restricted access to the Zabbix monitoring system as part of the service provider administration suite. Restricted access is granted because any modifications done to the Zabbix system can severely impact the quality of service and experience delivered via Apiculus.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Abou.title: About Estimates
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Abou.description: Estimates provide a better control to admin users and integrate seamlessly with IRP/ERP systems for effective invoice management. It reduces manual data entry errors by automating the invoicing process and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements for e-invoicing, such as generating Invoice Reference Numbers (IRN) and integrating with tax authorities.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Abou.title: About Invoice
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Abou.description: To view the invoice, navigate to the Billing and Financial section and select Invoices.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Addi.title: Adding One-time Charges and Discounts
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Addi.description: You can add charges and discounts manually as one-time subscriptions. It is beneficial for the use cases; if the invoice is generated and some discount or charge needs to be recorded for a customer, then you can do the same. This feature significantly enhances billing-related workflows.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Crea.title: Creating a Credit Note Against an Invoice
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Crea.description: You can create credit notes against paid or unpaid invoices. A credit note requires an invoice to be generated and present in the system. Credit notes can be used in cases such as disputed invoices, disputed charges, refunds etc., where editing an invoice is not financially possible.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Crea.title: Creating Credit Notes in Bulk
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Crea.description: Apiculus offers the means to upload credit notes in bulk (generate unique credit notes based on multiple linked invoices).

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Deac.title: Deactivating a Subscription
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Deac.description: Admins on Apiculus can deactivate subscriptions from the Admin Cloud Console which will, in turn, remove the resource(s) from the corresponding system(s).

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Over.title: Overview
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Over.description: The Billing and Financials section is designed to give complete details of the Apiculus billing system. This section gives administrators of Apiculus complete access to customers’ billing records, invoices, transactions, and other financial items supported on Apiculus.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Reco.title: Recording Tax Deductions
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Reco.description: You can configure tax deductions via a new section under Settings called Tax Deductions. This involves taxes deducted by customers while making a payment and is different from taxation on services which involves taxes charged on the services delivered. Admins can record quarterly tax deductions for all customer invoices. The Tax Deductions section lists all such tax deductions and allows for adding new items. The Tax Deductions feature is optional and needs to be enabled from the global settings, where admins also get the ability to define their country’s fiscal period and fiscal quarters.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Reco.title: Recording Transactions and Offline Payments
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Reco.description: You can record payments received outside the Apiculus system as Transactions using the Apiculus Admin Cloud Console. These payments may either be against invoices that are open or advance payments that adjust against invoices generated in future.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Rese.title: Resending Credit Notes
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Rese.description: You can resend credit notes to account owners from the account-level or global credit notes listings. Like invoices, this will only resend the email/file and not create a new credit note.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Reve.title: Reversing Transactions
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Reve.description: Transactions and payments, once received and recorded, can not be edited. In case of refunds or cancellations, a reverse entry must be made in the accounting books. All online and offline transactions (with the exception of credit notes) can be reversed by clicking on  Reverse Icon (reverse icon) in an account-level or global transactions listing, or by clicking the Reverse icon while viewing transaction details.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/Upda.title: Updating Subscription Charges
Administration/BillingandFinancials/Upda.description: The Apiculus Admin Cloud Console offers an easy way of handling subscription price changes. Admins can update subscription prices after a resource has been created and bring the new prices in effect from the current billing cycle.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.title: Viewing and Downloading Credit Notes
Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.description: Like invoices, credit notes are also stored as downloadable and printable .pdf files on the Apiculus management servers on the service provider premises. These can be downloaded and viewed in the same way as invoices, and can be accessed from account-level and global credit notes listings.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.title: Viewing and Downloading Invoices
Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.description: You can view all invoices that are generated on the Apiculus system. Apiculus stores invoices as downloadable and printable .pdf files that are stored on the Apiculus management server(s) on the service provider premises.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.title: Viewing Receipts and Acknowledgments
Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.description: Transaction receipts can be viewed for any transaction in an account-level or global listing by clicking on the Receipt (receipt icon). This will show the current status of any transaction along with transaction reference, associated information, details received from payment gateway etc.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.title: Viewing Tax Deductions
Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.description: You can view tax deductions recorded quarterly against historical invoices as per fiscal period configurations.

Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.title: Viewing Transaction Details
Administration/BillingandFinancials/View.description: Payments and transactions (online and offline), once recorded in the system, can be viewed in detail, including their amounts, dates, references, modes, narrations and apportionments.

Administration/ManagingQuickPlans/Adding.title: Adding Quick Plans to a Catalogue
Administration/ManagingQuickPlans/Adding.description: Once you’ve created your Quick Plans, you need to add them to all the catalogues that are present so that the quick plan is available for the end users to purchase. Here’s how:

Administration/ManagingQuickPlans/Creati.title: Creating Quick Plans
Administration/ManagingQuickPlans/Creati.description: After setting up your categories, the next step is to create Quick Plans. Quick Plans are pre-configured service packages that users can purchase.

Administration/ManagingQuickPlans/Settin.title: Setting Up a Quick Plan Category
Administration/ManagingQuickPlans/Settin.description: Creating a Quick Plan starts with setting up a Quick Plan Category, which helps in organising the quick plans.

Administration/MarketingManagement/Overv.title: Overview
Administration/MarketingManagement/Overv.description: To cater to the service provider’s marketing needs, service providers can create various promotional strategies as per their requirements. For example, Admins like marketing managers or super admin can create multiple discount codes and coupon codes as a part of the promotion strategy. These can be emailed to the existing customers, new customers, or demo accounts.

Administration/ResellerManagement/Creati.title: Creating and Updating Resellers
Administration/ResellerManagement/Creati.description: This feature requires the reseller module to be enabled.

Administration/ResellerManagement/Managi.title: Managing Billing for Resellers
Administration/ResellerManagement/Managi.description: Even with the Reseller module enabled and customers getting acquired via resellers, there is no impact on billing and availability of Services to the subscribers/customers. The points to consider here are:

Administration/ResellerManagement/Managi.title: Managing Reseller-tagged Accounts
Administration/ResellerManagement/Managi.description: This feature requires the reseller module to be enabled.

Administration/ResellerManagement/Overvi.title: Overview
Administration/ResellerManagement/Overvi.description: The reseller and channel management feature on Apiculus is aimed at service providers who have partners and customer acquisition channels. Service provider administrators can manage resellers, and each reseller can log in to view accounts that are tagged using their respective reseller codes.

Administration/ServicesManagement/Custom.title: Assigning Custom Catalogues to Customers
Administration/ServicesManagement/Custom.description: Custom Catalogues can be assigned to one or more customers using the Apiculus Admin Console. This is a guided workflow on the Apiculus Admin Console UI and can be done in simple steps.

Administration/ServicesManagement/Custom.title: Creating a Custom Catalogue
Administration/ServicesManagement/Custom.description: Custom Catalogues function similarly to Default Services, except that these are designated for specified customers. These catalogues have the following key characteristics and considerations:

Administration/ServicesManagement/Custom.description: Default Services and Custom Catalogues both offer the ability to enable or disable item availability. This works at three levels:

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: Instance protection safeguards virtual machine instances and workloads by creating backups using Acronis backup solutions.

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: Apiculus offers Virtual Firewall Instances as integrations with pfSense® and FortiGate VM.

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.id: Administration/ServicesManagement/Working with Default Services/Configuring Services/Networking services/ConfiguringVirtualPrivateClouds
Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.title: Configuring Virtual Private Clouds
Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is an isolated network inside a public cloud that allows organizations to manage their own network. The Default Services provide various options and pricing that help administrators to customize the configurations.

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.title: Configuring Block Volumes
Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: Apiculus Block Volumes are the underlying software-defined volume management layer that powers various storage-related or storage-driven cloud Services.

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.title: Configuring Object Storage
Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: In object storage, data is stored as discrete objects containing data, metadata, and a unique identifier. Apiculus delivers the service as an integration with Cloudian HyperStore. Admins can configure the Object storage from the Services section. To configure the Object Storage service, follow these steps:

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.title: Overview
Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: Default Services (DS) is the catalogue available to all subscribers, unless specified otherwise. In other words, the DS catalogue defines the default rules for availability of any supported Service via configurations of:

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.title: Working with Availability Zones
Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: Availability Zones (AZ) on Apiculus are a highly versatile and unique way of managing Service delivery and availability. Apiculus AZs are essentially control planes for underlying ISV, designed to handle a range of delivery scenarios. Apiculus AZs also take into account the limitations that an ISV may have (e.g., inability to manage multiple serviceable regions via a single management plane). In other words, Apiculus AZ can be seen as the 'delivery-level unification' of all underlying cloud services into a unified service topology.

Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.title: Working with Cloud Collections
Administration/ServicesManagement/Workin.description: A Cloud Collection (CC) on Apiculus is a grouping of repeating components (called Collection Items) that can be published to offer options/configurations for subscribers to choose from when purchasing a cloud resource. Apiculus CC can be based on a variety of factors, some examples of which are listed below:

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ac.title: Account Management Overview
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ac.description: Apiculus allows service providers to manage their subscriber/customer life cycles end to end. Subscriber account is one of the key entities that the entire solution is designed for. While Apiculus supports many features that impact the subscriber life cycles, this section focuses only on actions that can be performed within the Accounts section of the Apiculus service provider admin panel.

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ch.title: Changing Resource Limits for Subscribers
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ch.description: Subscriber accounts are created with default global resource limits that are defined in the Apache CloudStack orchestrator. These limits can be adjusted for subscriber accounts by navigating into the target account and selecting the Limits sub-section. These limits are vary for each cloud region that's configured to deliver services.

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ch.title: Changing Subscriber’s Primary Email
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ch.description: The Apiculus Admin Console allows you to change the customer’s primary email ID. The Primary email ID can be updated anytime through simple and verifiable steps with the notification sent to both the new and old email IDs.

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Im.title: Impersonating an Account
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Im.description: Apiculus provides a means for service provider support teams to login to a customer account and impersonate the subscriber. This is a useful tool for providing instant/on-call support.

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ma.title: Maintaining Relationship Personnel Information
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Ma.description: The Apiculus Cloud Console enables users to easily view information on designated relationship personnel, as specified by the service providers. Using the Admin Console, service providers can keep this information updated for each subscriber account.

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Up.title: Updating a Subscriber's Taxation Category
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Up.description: Service provider admins on Apiculus can change customers' taxation categories. This feature is useful for organisations acquiring special tax status, exemption status, or dealing with incorrect classification while creating an account.

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Up.title: Updating Credit Limit and Other Billing Information
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Up.description: Subscriber accounts' credit limits and other billing details can be updated from the Apiculus Admin Console.

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Wo.title: Working with Account States
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Wo.description: Apiculus enables customer life cycle management using states depending on where the relationship with individual customers stands in their life cycle. The following states are supported:

Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Wo.title: Working with KYC and Account Verification
Administration/SubscribersandAccounts/Wo.description: The Apiculus Admin Console allows to set up default KYC-based restrictions for both self-signup and admin-assisted accounts. When enabled, the respective account types will not be able to make purchases anything till their KYC/identity verification has been completed.

Administration/SupportManagement/End-to-.title: End-to-end Support Using OTRS
Administration/SupportManagement/End-to-.description: OTRS Community Edition is an ITIL-compliant open-source ticketing system. Along with basic ticket management, OTRS offers highly flexible workflow management capabilities, including and not limited to managing customer organisations, queues, custom SLAs, reports and also configuring and automating processes.

Administration/SupportManagement/Handlin.title: Handling Purchase and Provisioning Requests
Administration/SupportManagement/Handlin.description: Apiculus ships with the option to configure an Approval Mechanism for Services in the Catalogue(s). For all Services that require an approval from the admin, a Request is generated upon initiating purchase. Admins are notified of these requests via email, and can be accessed from Support & Operations > Requests from the main navigation menu.

Administration/SupportManagement/Overvie.title: Overview
Administration/SupportManagement/Overvie.description: The Apiculus Admin Console offers a way to offer basic support through the UI, in terms of diagnosis and other L1 tasks. In addition to the Admin Cloud Console, the Apiculus solution ships with OTRS Community Edition to enable service provider support teams to offer end-to-end SLA-based support via tickets to the end customers.

Administration/SupportManagement/Viewing.title: Viewing Activity Logs
Administration/SupportManagement/Viewing.description: The Apiculus Admin Console offers an easy-to-view activity logs which can be filtered by a range of criteria and download as .csv files.

Administration/TrialManagement/AboutTria.title: About Trials
Administration/TrialManagement/AboutTria.description: Apiculus offers two options for managing trial programmes, giving users the flexibility to choose how they want to experience the platform:

Administration/TrialManagement/Automatin.title: Automating Trial Enrollments
Administration/TrialManagement/Automatin.description: Apiculus automation policies support defining trial-specific triggers and actions. These can be used to create rich scenarios such as auto enrollments on signup, ramped-up trials, marketing email sequences or even used in conjunction with KYC and dunning triggers.

Administration/TrialManagement/Workingwi.title: Working with Account Trials
Administration/TrialManagement/Workingwi.description: Creating a New Account Trial Programme

Administration/TrialManagement/Workingwi.title: Working with Subscription Trials
Administration/TrialManagement/Workingwi.description: Creating a New Subscription Trial Programme

APIs/AccountManagement/DisableAccount.title: Disable Account
APIs/AccountManagement/DisableAccount.description: This will put the account in a disabled state and the subscriber will not be able to purchase any new resources or access any existing resources. Accounts in this state can only make payments which will automatically enable their accounts. This action is reversible using the Enable Account endpoint.

APIs/AccountManagement/EnableAccount.title: Enable Account
APIs/AccountManagement/EnableAccount.description: This will reverse the disabled or restricted account states and reactivate the account.

APIs/AccountManagement/RestrictAccount.title: Restrict Account
APIs/AccountManagement/RestrictAccount.description: This will put the account in a restricted state and the subscriber will not be able to purchase any new resources. This action is reversible using the Enable Account endpoint.

APIs/AccountManagement/TerminateAccount.title: Terminate Account
APIs/AccountManagement/TerminateAccount.description: This will put the account in a terminated state and the subscriber will not be able to do any action or view any information. Further, the account will be removed from all systems. This action is not reversible.

APIs/apioverview.title: API Overview
APIs/apioverview.description: Apiculus supports (basic) account and access management using REST API. The details are as follows:

APIs/AuthenticateSession.title: Authenticate Session (Login)
APIs/AuthenticateSession.description: This endpoint will authenticate the user session based on a valid email and password combination and return a valid token.

APIs/CloseSession.title: Close Session (Logout)
APIs/CloseSession.description: This will logout the authenticated user and close the session.

APIs/CreateAccount.title: Create Account
APIs/CreateAccount.description: Use this API endpoint to create a subscriber account.

APIs/Helpers/ACSKeysandRegions.title: ACS Keys and Regions
APIs/Helpers/ACSKeysandRegions.description: This endpoint will return the configured CloudStack regions and their keys based on the user’s token.

APIs/Helpers/CountryList.title: Country List
APIs/Helpers/CountryList.description: Use this API endpoint to get a list of countries for new account creation.

APIs/Helpers/StatesList.title: States List
APIs/Helpers/StatesList.description: Use this API endpoint to get the list of states/regions within a country for new account creation.

APIs/Helpers/TaxCategories.title: Tax Categories
APIs/Helpers/TaxCategories.description: This API will return available tax categories, tax types and currencies.

FAQs.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
FAQs.description: How are data durability and redundancy achieved with block volumes?Data redundancy is achieved at the storage level by design as the storage layer replicates Block Volume data across multiple storage devices and Availability Zones.

GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/Ad.title: Adding Custom Scripts
GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/Ad.description: Apiculus supports adding custom ` elements to the platform's HTML ` components for execution during rendering of the platform UI in browsers.

GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/Cu.title: Currency Localisation
GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/Cu.description: All currencies can be localised for display and financial logic. This helps deliver localised experiences based on local and cultural considerations.

GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/En.title: Enabling Reseller Module
GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/En.description: Apiculus ships with an experimental Reseller Module, which allows service providers to onboard subscribers/customers via a reseller partner. This is limited-functionality partner module, which enables the following:

GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/Ma.title: Managing Quick Plan Categories
GettingStarted/AdvancedConfigurations/Ma.description: Quick Plan categories allow to group Quick Plans visually and semantically under the Marketplace section on the Apiculus Cloud Console.

GettingStarted/BillingandFinancials/Conf.title: Configuring Additional Fields
GettingStarted/BillingandFinancials/Conf.description: You can find the taxation IDs and custom profile fields that need to be associated with a subscriber account under Administration > Settings > Financials & Business > Additional Fields and can be edited along with the order. Additionally, you can define minimum and maximum characters, and the delete taxation IDs.

GettingStarted/BillingandFinancials/Conf.title: Configuring Basic Billing
GettingStarted/BillingandFinancials/Conf.description: Service providers can easily set up key billing settings to manage invoice generation and credit terms for subscriber accounts.

GettingStarted/BillingandFinancials/Conf.title: Configuring Tax Deductions
GettingStarted/BillingandFinancials/Conf.description: Apiculus supports recording quarterly tax deductions for all customer invoices. The Tax Deductions section lists all such tax deductions and allows for adding new items. The Tax Deductions feature is optional and needs to be enabled from the global settings, where admins also get the ability to define their country’s fiscal period and fiscal quarters.

GettingStarted/BrandingandContent/Config.title: Configuring Custom Links
GettingStarted/BrandingandContent/Config.description: You can add custom links such as demo videos, help guides, etc. These custom links will be shown on the subscriber console. Note that these links will open up in a new tab/window when the subscriber clicks on them.

GettingStarted/BrandingandContent/Custom.title: Customising Brand and Graphic Assets
GettingStarted/BrandingandContent/Custom.description: You can customize your cloud to match your branding; 100% white labeling is possible. So right from the logo, fonts to the email footer are easily configurable.

GettingStarted/BrandingandContent/Publis.title: Publishing Standard Static Content
GettingStarted/BrandingandContent/Publis.description: You can configure the static content that the users can view from their portal. This content is helpful in information and lets the user know about the service provider’s EULA, SLA, privacy policy, etc. You need to type or paste the content into the respective sections to update it. If a policy is updated or a newer version is uploaded, then the updated date and time are captured, and this information is sent to the users via email.

GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ConfiguringSM.title: Configuring SMS Gateway
GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ConfiguringSM.description: The SMS gateway is a in-built Apiculus application that enables messages and notifications to be sent to end customers via SMS.

GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ConfiguringSy.title: Configuring System Emails using SMTP
GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ConfiguringSy.description: Apiculus Cloud Console is the top layer of the technology stack that is fine-tuned and Apiculus delivers all system-generated emails, messages and notifications using SMTP. Any valid email address can be set up as the system email sender using standard SMTP settings.

GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ManagingGoogl.title: Managing Google reCAPTCHA and Tag Manager
GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ManagingGoogl.description: Apiculus supports form protection and marketing stack integration as in-built apps using Google reCAPTCHA and Google Tag Manager (GTM), respectively.

GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ManagingPayme.title: Managing Payment Gateways
GettingStarted/InBuiltApps/ManagingPayme.description: Apiculus Admin Console allows service providers to use any of the available payment gateways to receive payments in their preferred currency. Payments is an in-built app that allows one or more payment gateways to be enabled if the service provider wishes to offer multiple options to their subscriber users.

GettingStarted/LoginConfigurations/Confi.title: Configuring Password Policies
GettingStarted/LoginConfigurations/Confi.description: Apiculus platform admins can set up password policies from Administration > Settings >  Passwords. A host of controls is available to set up password policies, including:

GettingStarted/LoginConfigurations/Login.title: Login Security and 2FA
GettingStarted/LoginConfigurations/Login.description: You can set up two-factor authentication (2FA) for subscribers and admins separately. If selected, a one-time password (OTP) is sent via email to the users attempting to log in. Additionally, if an SMS gateway is enabled and configured, the OTP is also be sent to the user's registered mobile phone.

GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Co.title: Configuring Automation Policies
GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Co.description: Apiculus allows service providers to define automation policies and workflows to handle scenarios around invoice reminders, non-payments, and customer identity verification.

GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Ge.title: General Platform Settings
GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Ge.description: Configure essential platform settings like mobile access, VM deletion, and session timeouts for both subscribers and admins. Navigate to Administration > Settings > General to adjust these options.

GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Ma.title: Managing Service Provider Profile
GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Ma.description: The profile consists of all the service provider (SP) details; admins can configure all the details straight from the name of the cloud they will offer as to the address.

GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Ma.title: Managing Service Provider Admin Users
GettingStarted/PlatformAdministration/Ma.description: Navigate to Administration > Settings > Admin Users to view all the administrators with their roles. In addition, specific service provider (SP) teams can be onboarded with restricted administrative access using Service Provider Admin RBAC. RBAC is a highly flexible access management system that can be configured differently for each service provider. Service providers can specify the actions allowed for different user types, and the entire setup can be configured accordingly.

GettingStarted/SignupConfigurations/Conf.title: Configuring Account Defaults
GettingStarted/SignupConfigurations/Conf.description: Account defaults include the various configurations that are applied once an account is created successfully. For example, it can be KYC needed for self-signup accounts, or it can be the minimum or maximum number associated with the account as required or any other check like profile check enabled.

GettingStarted/SignupConfigurations/Rest.title: Restricting Free Email Domains
GettingStarted/SignupConfigurations/Rest.description: Apiculus allows restricting account signups from free email providers and other custom domains.

GettingStarted/SignupConfigurations/Sign.title: Signup Settings
GettingStarted/SignupConfigurations/Sign.description: You can customise signup settings as per your need. For example, you want that on an account signup, you want to be notified, (receive an email) or your customer’s account ID should start with a particular choice of sequence.

intro.title: Welcome
intro.description: Apiculus is an end-to-end cloud-as-a-service solution that addresses the technology, business enablement and customer experience needs of regional, domestic and edge cloud service providers. Apiculus presents itself as an all-in-one technology platform that combines cloud service monetisation, infrastructure management and customer life cycle management, and offers an integrated solution for cloud providers in edge markets.

Introduction/AccessCentral.title: Apiculus Access Central
Introduction/AccessCentral.description: Apiculus offers a single login page for subscribers and admins alike, and redirects based on the type of user who is logging in (subscriber, reseller, admin).

Introduction/BillingConcepts.title: Billing Concepts
Introduction/BillingConcepts.description: Billing on Apiculus Cloud works on a subscription model. In line with modern-day apps and services, subscription billing models are an efficient way to deliver service value as a pay-as-you-go license. This not only eliminates heavy upfront costs for any service purchase, but also gives you the flexibility to cancel at any time and pay for only the amount or duration of use.

Introduction/BuildingBlocks.title: Building Blocks
Introduction/BuildingBlocks.description: Apiculus Cloud Console is the topmost layer on a technology stack that is fine-tuned and optimised for performance, features and experience. The Apiculus solution uses Enterprise-grade components to handle various facets of infrastructure management and service delivery.

Introduction/SupportedPaymentGateways.title: Supported Payment Gateways
Introduction/SupportedPaymentGateways.description: The following payment gateways are supported on Apiculus to receive payments:

Introduction/SupportedSMSGateways.title: Supported SMS Gateways
Introduction/SupportedSMSGateways.description: Apiculus supports SMS gateways that can be used for sending SMS notifications, sending one-time passwords (OTP) and verifying mobile number ownership. The following SMS gateways are supported:

ResettingPassword.title: Resetting Password
ResettingPassword.description: Forgotten passwords can be reset by clicking on I have forgotten my password on Access Central. If the email entered is valid and exists on the system, instructions to set a new password will be sent by email.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusAu.title: About Apiculus Authoritative Nameserver
ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusAu.description: Apiculus Authoritative Nameserver (A53) is a DNS management solution built in to the Apiculus stack. A53 uses PowerDNS to manage all authoritative DNS operations and also includes Poweradmin as the administrative and management sub-layer.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusAu.title: DNS Requirements
ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusAu.description: A53 is a nameserver system. Therefore, enabling the Service requires certain prerequisites to be handled at the domain level.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusAu.title: System Requirements
ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusAu.description: Apiculus Authoritative Nameserver (A53) is deployed on a dedicated virtual machine within the Apiculus management stack for optimal performance and security.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusOb.title: About Apiculus Object Storage
ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusOb.description: Apiculus Object Storage (AS3) provides scalable and flexible storage for unstructured data in the form of objects. In object storage, data is stored as discrete objects containing data, metadata, and a unique identifier. Apiculus delivers the service as an integration with Cloudian HyperStore.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusOb.title: Feature List and Integration Details
ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusOb.description: AS3 is delivered as an integration with Cloudian HyperStore. The integration functions using API-driven checkpoints.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusOb.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
ServicesandSystemRequirements/ApiculusOb.description: What is object storage in the context of Apiculus?Object storage is a scalable and durable storage solution for managing unstructured data such as files, images, videos, and backups.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/AWSLinkage.title: Integration Details
ServicesandSystemRequirements/AWSLinkage.description: AWS Linkage is delivered as an integration with Amazon Web Services. The integration functions using API-driven checkpoints.

ServicesandSystemRequirements/LoadBalanc.title: About Load Balancer Instances
ServicesandSystemRequirements/LoadBalanc.description: The Apiculus Load Balancer Instance Service enables you to create the instances using NetScaler VPX as a base image. By using Apiculus Load Balancer Instances, organisations can distribute incoming network traffic across multiple servers or resources to ensure optimal performance, high availability, and scalability of applications and services.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/AboutApiculusA.title: About Apiculus Account Centre
Subscribers/AccountCentre/AboutApiculusA.description: The Account Centre on the Apiculus Cloud Console is a non-technical (commercial and support) dashboard used for viewing and managing all non-technical aspects of your Apiculus Cloud account. You can access the Account Centre by clicking on any item in the user menu at the top-right, the help/support icon in the header, or the wallet icon in the header.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/InvoicesandCre.title: Invoices and Credit Notes
Subscribers/AccountCentre/InvoicesandCre.description: Invoices are generated at the end of each billing cycle for the services consumed during the billing cycle. Invoices can be accessed from the Billing > Invoices & Credit Notes section on the main navigation panel.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/Organisation-A.title: Organisation/Account Profile
Subscribers/AccountCentre/Organisation-A.description: Using the Profile section, you can manage your organisation account profile on Apiculus Cloud. Subscribers/AccountCentre/StandardAgreem.description: Standard agreements and documents are available on the Apiculus Cloud Console Account Centre. Whenever any of these documents is amended or updated, you will be prompted to accept the modified documents on your next login.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsa.title: Subscriptions and Services
Subscribers/AccountCentre/Subscriptionsa.description: A subscription is a record created whenever a new resource is purchased or a service is activated. In other words, a subscription is a financial ‘agreement’ that links the item that has been purchased, its value, and its renewal frequency.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/SupportandTick.title: Support and Tickets
Subscribers/AccountCentre/SupportandTick.description: Apiculus Cloud provides SLA-driven support to all subscriber customers in accordance with the terms and conditions outlined in the Service Level Agreement. Subscribers/AccountCentre/TeamandChildUs.title: Team and Child User Management
Subscribers/AccountCentre/TeamandChildUs.description: Team members or child users can be onboarded using the TEAM section from the Account Centre, allows you to add billing, technical, or other admin users who can log in to your account and perform operations.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/UnbilledCharge.title: Unbilled Charges
Subscribers/AccountCentre/UnbilledCharge.description: All subscriptions incur prorated charges during an account’s billing cycle. These charges, also known as unbilled charges, can be accessed from Billing > Unbilled Charges in the main navigation panel.

Subscribers/AccountCentre/WalletandTrans.title: Wallet and Transactions
Subscribers/AccountCentre/WalletandTrans.description: For all practical purposes, the service balance acts as your wallet balance or purchase capacity at any given point of time. The service balance is an aggregation of your credit limit, total payments made, and the total charges incurred on your account. Your service balance is always displayed on the top bar and gets updated in real-time when a transaction or a charge is recorded.

The Cloud Console user journeys are simple, visual, and information-rich which makes managing cloud resources extremely satisfying.

Subscribers/Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutos.title: About Autoscale
Subscribers/Compute/Autoscale/AboutAutos.description: The Autoscale feature allows a system to adjust its computing resources based on the changing workload automatically. In virtual machines or instances, autoscaling helps ensure that an application or service has the right amount of resources to handle varying levels of demand efficiently. The key concept behind autoscaling is dynamic resource allocation. When demand for a particular application or service increases, autoscaling automatically adds more computing resources (such as instances or virtual machines) to handle the load. Conversely, during periods of lower demand, autoscaling reduces the number of resources to optimize cost and efficiency.

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.title: Port Forwarding for VM via VNF
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.description: This section outlines the steps to configure port forwarding for a Virtual Machine (VM) using a Virtual Network Function (VNF) in a cloud environment. Port forwarding allows external clients to securely access services (for example SSH, HTTP) hosted on the VM by mapping ports from the VNF’s public IP to the VM’s private IP. This approach improves network segmentation and control by routing all incoming traffic through the VNF layer.

Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.title: Port Forwarding with Public IP
Subscribers/Networking/NATGateways/PortF.description: In Apiculus cloud platform, an additional Public IP enables you to expose internal services hosted on virtual machines (VMs) to the internet by creating dedicated port forwarding rules. This approach is especially useful when multiple external IPs are needed for services such as SSH, HTTP, or custom applications. By assigning specific public IPs to particular services or VMs, you can achieve greater control, flexibility, and network segmentation.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.title: Accessing the VFI Control Panel
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.description: The Virtual Firewall Service is delivered as an integration using OS images pre-loaded with pfSense or FortiGate VM.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.title: Adding Instances to a VFI-based Network
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/A.description: Instances can be created and placed in the L2-LAN (Free Networks) created by the Virtual Firewall. These Instances use special Linux OS images, which are available as a separate OS Collection. Users can create Linux Instances, and choose the appropriate options/controls to create an Instance in the Virtual Firewall network.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/D.title: Deploying VNF and VM Within VNF
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/D.description: This section provides a step-by-step guide for deploying a Virtual Network Function (VNF)—specifically a virtual firewall—and provisioning a Virtual Machine (VM) behind it using the Apiculus Cloud platform. This setup allows users to route VM traffic through the VNF, enabling secure and segmented network environments.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.title: About Firewall Instances
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualFirewall/F.description: Virtual Firewalls enables you to use Virtual Firewalls in a Virtualized Network Function (VNF) style, which include support for multiple VLANs and Public IPv4 addresses and automated service activation. This allows for more robust firewall management capabilities.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: About Virtual Private Clouds
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: Details about Virtual Private Cloud operations and actions can be found in their respective sections.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Creating VPC Subnets and Tiers
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: VPCs follow the convention of 3-tiered architectures, with web, app, and DB tiers forming the norm. You can, however, configure these tiers to suit your application architecture or just follow the common convention.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: IPv4 Addresses and VPC
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: IPv4 Addresses are an integral part of using VPC networking, and need to be used to access various components of the VPC. By default, a public IPv4 Address is assigned to the VR which can communicate through the internet to transmit traffic to/from the VR. This IPv4 can also be used for configuring remote access (L2TP) and site-to-site (IPSec) VPN connections.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Managing Access Control on VPC Subnets
Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: Access control policies can be created using Access Control Lists (ACL) and configuring rules within these ACL (called ACL Rules). An ACL can then be applied to any tier within the VPC. These policies govern what traffic is allowed or restricted into and from your VPC network tiers.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: Overview

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.description: The Reconfigure section/tab lists your current subscription details and allows you to reconfigure the networking pack or switch between hourly and monthly pricing.

Subscribers/Networking/VirtualPrivateClo.title: VPC Management and Basic Operations

Subscribers/SigningUp.description: To get started with the Yntraa cloud platform, new users must create an account through a straightforward sign-up process. Whether you're accessing the platform for the first time or looking to register for cloud services, the platform offers a user-friendly interface designed to make account creation simple and secure. Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBl.title: About Block Volumes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/AboutBl.description: Apiculus Block Volumes are the underlying software-defined volume management layer that powers various storage-related or storage-driven cloud services. Block Volumes can be used with Linux and Windows instances as root and data disks, with Kubernetes as persistent storage, or simply as a storehouse for snapshots and images.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Creatin.title: Creating Data Disk
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Creatin.description: To create a Block Volume data disk, follow the below steps:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Operations
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: To view all available Block Volume operations, navigate to the Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Operations tab.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Overview
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Navigate to the Overview tab to view the following details:

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Resize the Block Volume
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: To resize an available Block Volumes, navigate to Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Resize tab.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Creating Automated Snapshot Schedules
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Apiculus Cloud Console users can create snapshot policies for their root and data disks. These schedules enable for strategic management of data retention policies and can be used as an alternative backup solution. To create or manage the Snapshot schedules, navigate to the Schedules section of disk details.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.title: Working with Volume Snapshots
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Details.description: Apiculus Block Volumes service provides extensive functionality for managing volume snapshots. Snapshots are point-in-time 'images' of a volume’s contents and can be used as a restoration point for the parent volume. The following sections outline all available snapshot functions and capabilities in the Apiculus Cloud Console.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBl.title: Using Block Volumes with Kubernetes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/UsingBl.description: Apiculus Kubernetes (AK8s) also uses the underlying Block Volumes service to create persistent volumes for Kubernetes clusters. Apiculus Cloud Console users can specify the root disk size for each cluster node (control and worker), and the Block Volume service creates the nodes in the cluster with a root disk of the specified size.

Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Working.title: Working with Instance Volumes
Subscribers/Storage/BlockVolumes/Working.description: Each Instance on Apiculus (Linux or Windows) includes a 'root' disk or volume. This is the primary disk partition on the Instance, where the operating system, data, components and files reside on the root disk.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.title: About Apiculus Object Storage
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.description: Apiculus Object Storage (AS3) provides scalable and flexible storage for unstructured data in the form of objects. In object storage, data is stored as discrete objects containing data, metadata, and a unique identifier. Apiculus delivers the service through an integration with Cloudian HyperStore.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.title: Creating Object Storage Bucket
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.description: An Object Storage Bucket is where you store your files in the cloud. Creating an Object Storage Bucket is the first step to organize, access, and manage your data easily.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.title: Feature List and Integration Details
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.description: AS3 is delivered as an integration with Cloudian HyperStore. The integration functions using API-driven checkpoints.

Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
Subscribers/Storage/ObjectStorageBucket/.description: What is object storage in the context of Apiculus?object storage is a scalable and durable storage solution for managing unstructured data such as files, images, videos, and backups.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AboutTools.title: About Tools and Utilities
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/AboutTools.description: The Tools and Utilities sections in the Apiculus Cloud Console keep a record of cloud management utilities and assets that might be needed to use specific sections of the Apiculus-powered cloud.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCu.title: Managing Custom Templates and Images
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingCu.description: Custom OS templates can be created from a Volume Snapshot. To view custom templates, navigate to Tools and Utilities > My Images. The following details are displayed:

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingIn.title: Managing Instance and Volume Snapshots
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingIn.description: Snapshots use the Apiculus Block Volumes service and occupy billable storage space.

Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSS.title: Managing SSH Keys and Key Pairs for Linux Instances
Subscribers/ToolsandUtilities/ManagingSS.description: The Apiculus Cloud Console allows you to create secure shell connections to Instances in your account by using SSH keys, in addition to using Instance passwords. Since passwords can be easily compromised, SSH keys provide a more secure alternative. These keys function as encrypted signatures and work only when their public and private components match.

WalletandTransactions/WalletandTransacti.title: Wallet and Transactions
WalletandTransactions/WalletandTransacti.description: The Wallet and Transactions section gives users a quick view of their account balance and transaction history. It shows available funds, including wallet and voucher balances, and offers basic tools to search, filter, and download transaction records. This section helps users keep track of their billing and payments in one place.
